title: TC-FITTEST-001: Fit Test completes successfully without interruption (baseline)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on the connected phone.
Tap the connected device name (e.g., Voyager Free 60+ UC, Poly Voyager Surround 80, or supported earbuds).
Navigate to Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
Wait for the fit test to complete without pressing any earbud buttons.
Observe the fit test result screen.
expected: Fit Test completes successfully. A result screen is displayed showing the fit quality (e.g., 'Good Fit' or 'Adjust Fit') for each earbud. No interruption or premature cancellation occurs.

title: TC-FITTEST-002: Fit Test interrupted by single press on primary earbud button during playback
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap connected device → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
While the fit test audio is playing (within 2-3 seconds of starting), perform a single press of the hardware button on the PRIMARY earbud (the earbud designated as primary in the device UI).
Observe the Fit Test screen and the audio playback state.
expected: The Fit Test is interrupted/cancelled by the button press. The fit test audio stops. The app may return to the Fit Test start screen or show a 'Cancelled'/'Interrupted' state. The button press should not cause the app to crash or become unresponsive.

title: TC-FITTEST-003: Fit Test interrupted by single press on non-primary earbud button during playback
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap connected device → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
While the fit test audio is playing (within 2-3 seconds of starting), perform a single press of the hardware button on the NON-PRIMARY earbud (the other earbud).
Observe the Fit Test screen and the audio playback state.
expected: The Fit Test is interrupted/cancelled by the button press on the non-primary earbud. The fit test audio stops. Behavior should be consistent with pressing the primary earbud button — no crash, no hang, and the app returns to a stable state.

title: TC-FITTEST-004: Fit Test interrupted by double press on primary earbud during playback
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap connected device → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
While the fit test audio is playing, perform a double press of the hardware button on the PRIMARY earbud.
Observe the Fit Test screen and audio state.
expected: The Fit Test is interrupted/cancelled. The double press action (which may normally trigger next track or other function) takes precedence and stops the fit test. App should not crash and should return to a stable state.

title: TC-FITTEST-005: Fit Test interrupted by long press on primary earbud during playback
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap connected device → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
While the fit test audio is playing, perform a long press (hold for ~2 seconds) of the hardware button on the PRIMARY earbud.
Observe the Fit Test screen and audio state.
expected: The Fit Test is interrupted/cancelled. The long press action (which may normally trigger voice assistant or other function) takes precedence. App should not crash and should return to a stable state.

title: TC-FITTEST-006: Fit Test interrupted by button press on both earbuds simultaneously
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap connected device → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
While the fit test audio is playing, press the hardware button on BOTH earbuds simultaneously.
Observe the Fit Test screen and audio state.
expected: The Fit Test is interrupted/cancelled. The app handles simultaneous button presses gracefully — no crash, no hang, no undefined state. The app returns to a stable Fit Test start screen or shows a cancelled state.

title: TC-FITTEST-007: Fit Test result consistency after multiple start/interrupt cycles
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap connected device → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
Immediately press the PRIMARY earbud button to interrupt (within 1 second).
Wait 3 seconds for the app to settle.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' again to restart the fit test.
Let the fit test run to completion without interruption this time.
Observe the fit test result.
expected: After the interruption, the second Fit Test runs to completion successfully and displays a valid fit result (e.g., 'Good Fit' or 'Adjust Fit'). The interruption does not corrupt the fit test state for subsequent attempts. No crash or UI freeze occurs.

title: TC-FITTEST-008: Fit Test interrupted at the exact moment of completion
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap connected device → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
As the fit test audio is finishing (last ~1 second of playback), press the PRIMARY earbud button.
Observe the Fit Test result screen and audio state.
expected: The Fit Test either completes and shows the result, or is cancelled by the button press. In either case, the app should not crash, freeze, or show a partial/blank result screen. The app should be in a stable state (either result displayed or Fit Test start screen).

title: TC-FITTEST-009: Fit Test interrupted by rapid successive button presses on primary earbud
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap connected device → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
While the fit test audio is playing, rapidly press the PRIMARY earbud button 5 times in quick succession.
Observe the Fit Test screen and audio state.
expected: The Fit Test is interrupted/cancelled by the first button press. The subsequent rapid presses do not cause any crash, double-cancellation error, or UI glitch. The app remains stable on the Fit Test start screen or cancelled state.

title: TC-FITTEST-010: Fit Test interrupted by button press, then immediately restarted and interrupted again
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap connected device → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
Press PRIMARY earbud button to interrupt (within 2 seconds).
Immediately (within 1 second) tap 'Start Fit Test' again.
While the second fit test audio is playing, press the NON-PRIMARY earbud button to interrupt.
Observe the Fit Test screen after the second interruption.
expected: Both interruptions are handled cleanly. After the second interruption, the app returns to a stable Fit Test start screen. No crash, no UI corruption, no residual audio playback. The app is ready for another Fit Test attempt.

title: TC-FITTEST-011: Fit Test with earbud button press during the 'Analyzing' phase (post-playback)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap connected device → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
Wait for the fit test audio to finish playing (the app transitions to an 'Analyzing...' or processing state).
During the 'Analyzing...' phase, press the PRIMARY earbud button.
Observe the Fit Test result screen.
expected: The Fit Test should complete its analysis and display the result. The button press during the analysis phase should not cancel the result or cause a crash. The fit result should be displayed correctly.

title: TC-FITTEST-012: Fit Test interrupted by earbud button press while media is playing in background
steps: Start playing music or a podcast on the connected phone (e.g., Spotify, YouTube Music).
Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap connected device → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback (media should pause or duck).
While the fit test audio is playing, press the PRIMARY earbud button.
Observe the Fit Test screen, audio state, and whether background media resumes.
expected: The Fit Test is interrupted/cancelled by the button press. After interruption, the background media should resume playing normally (or return to its previous state). The app should not crash and the Fit Test screen should return to the start state.

title: TC-FITTEST-013: Fit Test interrupted by earbud button press during a phone call
steps: Initiate or receive a phone call on the connected phone while earbuds are connected.
While on the call, open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap connected device → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
While the fit test audio is playing, press the PRIMARY earbud button.
Observe the Fit Test screen, call state, and audio behavior.
expected: The Fit Test is interrupted/cancelled by the button press. The phone call should remain active and not be dropped. The call audio should return to normal after the fit test interruption. No crash or call disconnection occurs.

title: TC-FITTEST-014: Fit Test result screen dismissed by earbud button press after successful completion
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap connected device → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' and let it complete without interruption.
On the Fit Test result screen (showing 'Good Fit' or 'Adjust Fit'), press the PRIMARY earbud button.
Observe the app behavior.
expected: The Fit Test result screen should remain displayed. The earbud button press should not dismiss the result screen or trigger any unintended action. The result should persist until the user manually navigates away.

title: TC-FITTEST-015: Fit Test state after app is backgrounded and restored during button-press interruption
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap connected device → Audio Settings → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test' to begin the fit test audio playback.
While the fit test audio is playing, press the PRIMARY earbud button to interrupt.
Immediately press the Home button (or gesture) to background the app.
Wait 5 seconds, then reopen the Poly Studio Mobile app from the recent apps list or home screen.
Observe the Fit Test screen state.
expected: The app should restore to a stable state — either the Fit Test start screen or the cancelled state. No crash, no frozen UI, no residual 'in-progress' state. The app should be fully responsive and allow starting a new Fit Test.
